text:
byJACKSON WALKER | The National Desk

TOPICS:

WASHINGTON (TND) —A media outlet linked to the terrorist group ISIS released a poster Thursday which appeared to threaten a direct attack on Washington D.C.

The poster, disseminated by the Al-Azaim Foundation and obtained by the Middle East Media Research Institute (MEMRI), shows the U.S. Capitol in the center of red crosshairs. A figure in the foreground wearing a military uniform can be seen holding a grenade in a clenched fist.

“You are next” the poster reads.

A similar image released during the 2024 Olympics appeared to warn of an attack in Paris. That image depicted a drone delivering a "gift" to the Eiffel Tower.

"Lone wolves' Olympics have begun with the will of Allah,” the caption of that image read.

U.S. Central Command, or "Centcom," released a statement in July blaming ISIS for 153 attacks in Iraq and Syria. The terrorist group, Centcom said, was on pace to more than double the total number of attacks it claimed responsibility for in 2023.

"The media outlet’s messaging, disseminated in multiple languages and addressing many audiences, is currently seen as the spearhead of anti-West sentiment promoting ISIS’s violent ideology," MEMRI's Jihad and Terrorism Threat Monitor team wrote in a statement. "It is essential to note that inciting attacks in the West is linked to a global environment marked by an ongoing Hamas-Israel war, but also this week’s anniversary of the 9/11 attacks."

The threat follows an August celebration by the Taliban marking three years since its return to power in Afghanistan. Images circulating social media showed uniformed troops flying helicopters and parading a convoy of armored trucks through what was once the Bagram Airfield. The hardware had reportedly been left abandoned since the U.S. withdrew from Afghanistan in 2021.

A retired military leader formerly responsible for overseeing the 2021 U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan admitted in March he did not know the number of Americans who were left behind in the process.

"I'll be candid — I don't know the exact number of Americans that were left behind because the starting number was never clear,” former Joint Chiefs of Staff Chair Gen. Mark Milley said.

Milley also revealed he lacked information on the number of Afghans who served with U.S. forces. Some of these individuals, he believed, were killed “in some pretty brutal ways.”

FollowJackson Walker on X at @_jlwalker_ for the latest trending national news. Have a news tip? Send it to jacwalker@sbgtv.com.
title: 'You are next': Pro-ISIS group creates poster targeting US Capitol
author: JACKSON WALKER | The National Desk
description: A similar image released during the 2024 Olympics appeared to warn of an attack in Paris.
